name: Delicata
image_alt: Cream and green striped oblong winter squash
description: A small, oblong winter squash with cream-colored skin striped in green and orange. The flesh is sweet, dense, and nutty — often compared to sweet potato. The thin, edible skin is one of its best features.
history: First introduced in 1894 by Peter Henderson & Co. of New York. Fell out of commercial favor in the mid-20th century but was revived by small farms and seed savers in the 1990s as interest in heirloom vegetables grew.

good.reason: Classic Three Sisters — provides structure for vines

seasonal_quirks: Unlike most winter squash, Delicata does not store for many months — expect 3 months maximum. The thin skin that makes it so appealing also limits shelf life. Flavor sweetens after a few weeks of curing.
harvest_cues: Skin should be fully cream-colored with no green remaining. The stem should be dry and corky. Skin won't dent with a fingernail when ripe.
variety_notes: Each plant produces 4–6 fruits, making yield calculation easy. The single-serving size (about 1 lb each) makes it practical for weeknight cooking — no cutting a huge squash.
preservation: Cure at room temperature for 1–2 weeks after harvest. Store in a cool, dry place up to 3 months. Does not freeze as well as thicker-fleshed types.
